block_01: Mastercard & Visa have announced they will be reducing overseas fees by up to 40%.
block_02: According to the European Commission the deal will lead to "lower prices for European retailers to do business" this should lead to lower prices for tourists to the EU. The new rates are expected to come into effect on the 19th October 2019, & will stand for five years.
block_03: Earlier in the year, Mastercard was fined $650 million, for anti-competitive behaviour. They stood accused of preventing retailers of using cheaper banking services outside their home country.
block_04: Going forward, both Mastercard & Visa will be subject to a fine of up to 10% of their global turnover if they fail to meet their commitments.
